claim: Rumen Radev is still the likeliest single named outcome, but the market's 0.9035 price looks materially too high for a parliamentary, coalition-dependent, formally-sworn-in contract with explicit caretaker exclusion and a real deadlock/'Other' path. My post-synthesis view is that Radev is favored but not close to locked.

frontmatter.contract_ambiguity_reason: Market closes before post-election swearing-in, so pre-resolution reporting vs later official confirmation timing could create interpretation noise, though core what-counts rule is clear.
independently_verified_points: Caretaker prime ministers are explicitly excluded by the contract
Official Bulgarian government materials indicate the pre-election PM is a caretaker PM
Radev resigned the presidency and is actively leading/associated with Progressive Bulgaria in the 2026 election cycle
Bulgaria's parliamentary fragmentation makes coalition formation, not headline candidacy alone, the key bottleneck
frontmatter.verification_gap_summary: No independently verified coalition whip-count or partner-commitment evidence shows Radev already has a dominant path to formal investiture.
frontmatter.best_countercase_summary: The market may reflect stronger local or trader information that Radev is already the de facto coalition compromise and therefore much closer to a lock than public sourcing shows.
frontmatter.main_reason_for_disagreement: How much weight to put on unverified but apparently strong market confidence versus publicly auditable coalition-path evidence.
frontmatter.resolution_mechanics_summary: Only the first non-caretaker person officially sworn in as PM after the 2026 election counts; if none is appointed by 2027-03-31 ET, the market resolves Other.
frontmatter.freshness_driver: Post-election seat distribution and coalition-partner signals can rapidly change the probability before any formal swearing-in.
decision_blockers: No strong independent verification that Radev already has a secured or near-secured governing coalition
Coalition arithmetic in a fragmented parliament could still produce another PM or delay resolution
Market confidence may embed local information not fully visible in accessible public sources
frontmatter.next_checkpoint: Official results and first credible coalition-partner commitments immediately after the 2026-04-19 election.

sections.Alpha summary: Market-implied probability is 0.9035; my syndicated range is 0.62 to 0.74. That is a meaningful below-market view and potentially actionable only if one believes public evidence is not missing a major local-information edge. The likely mispricing is favorite-versus-certainty compression: Radev may be the frontrunner, but the market appears to underprice coalition and formal investiture risk.
sections.Input coverage: All five personas were available: base-rate, catalyst-hunter, market-implied, risk-manager, and variant-view. None were missing. I used the raw persona findings as canonical inputs and checked selected source-note artifacts for provenance and verification quality. Coverage is complete because all planned personas arrived, though candidate-specific coalition evidence remains thinner than ideal.
sections.Market-implied baseline: The synthesis baseline is the 0.9035 market price at 2026-04-13T21:06:05Z. Across personas, the swarm baseline centered well below market, with four lanes clustering in the mid/high 60s to low 70s and one bearish base-rate outlier at 0.35.
sections.Syndicated probability estimate: My final post-synthesis estimate is 0.62 to 0.74. Radev remains the likeliest single person, but I do not see enough independently verified coalition-path evidence to justify anything close to 90%+ in a fragmented parliamentary system where only formal swearing-in counts.
sections.Difference from swarm-implied center: This is broadly in line with the swarm-implied center around the high 0.60s, but I narrow away from the base-rate persona's 0.35 because synthesis-stage verification did confirm that Radev is a real, active contender rather than a category-error candidate. I did not move up toward market because the key missing piece—credible coalition-lock evidence—remained missing.
sections.Agreement or disagreement with market: I disagree with the market on certainty, not on favorite status. The market may be right that Radev is the modal outcome, but 0.9035 implies that most coalition and timing risk is already gone. The available public evidence does not support that level of confidence.
sections.Independent verification of edge: Verification quality is medium. The synthesis independently confirmed several critical mechanics: caretaker exclusion, current caretaker status, election timing, Radev's resignation from the presidency, and the parliamentary coalition bottleneck. That is enough to reject a simplistic near-lock interpretation. But verification did not reach the decisive question of whether Radev already has secured coalition support or a clearly dominant investiture path, so the edge is only partially verified.
sections.Compression toward market due to verific: No. I did not compress meaningfully toward the market because the additional verification did not uncover strong pro-market evidence; if anything, it reinforced that the contract is narrower and more coalition-dependent than a 90%+ price suggests. I also did not stay near the most bearish swarm tail because verification did support Radev as a serious, plausible frontrunner.
sections.Timing and catalyst posture: The next major catalyst is the 2026-04-19 election followed immediately by seat allocation and coalition bargaining. The edge is more likely to compress quickly if credible coalition commitments for Radev appear, and more likely to widen if fragmented results or exclusion coalitions emerge. Waiting for post-election coalition signals is likely to improve decision quality materially.
sections.Decision blockers: Main blockers are lack of independently verified coalition commitments, uncertainty about whether anti-Radev coalitions can coalesce, and the possibility that market pricing embeds local information not visible in accessible sourcing. This is not a blocker to having a view, but it is a blocker to high-conviction anti-market sizing.
sections.Implication for the question: The best current interpretation is: probably yes, but with much more failure path than the market implies. Radev should be treated as favored, not effectively certain, to become the next qualifying sworn-in PM.
sections.Consensus across personas: There was strong cross-persona agreement that the contract excludes caretaker PMs, that the resolving event is formal swearing-in rather than election-night leadership, that Bulgaria's fragmented parliamentary system makes coalition arithmetic central, and that Radev is a genuine leading contender but the market likely overstates certainty. Four of five lanes clustered around a favorite-but-below-market view.
sections.Key disagreements across personas: The main disagreement was weighting-based and market-pricing based: how far below market to go given incomplete candidate-specific evidence. Base-rate treated fragmentation and deadlock risk as dominant and landed at 0.35. The other four personas gave more weight to evidence that Radev is a real, leading contender and clustered around 0.65-0.72. A secondary disagreement was factual-weighting around how much confidence to place in contextual reporting on Progressive Bulgaria's strength versus absence of hard coalition commitments.
sections.Best countercase: Best countercase: the market's 90%+ price may reflect stronger local information than the accessible sources captured, such as party-level knowledge that Radev is already the accepted coalition focal point and that alternatives are not viable. The catalyst-hunter and variant-view lanes preserved this best.
sections.Encapsulated assumptions: Shared assumptions: Radev is a real contender; caretaker PMs do not count; formal swearing-in is what resolves; coalition bargaining matters materially. Contested assumptions: whether Radev is already coalition-central enough to justify very high odds; whether market pricing reflects hidden high-quality local information. Fragile assumptions: that current public polling/seat context remained stable into election week and that no pre-election coalition lock existed outside accessible sourcing.
sections.Encapsulated evidence map: Strongest supporting evidence: official/credible reporting that Radev resigned the presidency and leads or is associated with Progressive Bulgaria; market and institutional logic that he is the main focal candidate; official confirmation that the current PM is only caretaker. Strongest contradictory evidence: fragmented parliament, repeated coalition failures since 2021, contextual polling suggesting first place but far from majority, and absence of verified coalition commitments. Authoritative source-of-truth evidence: contract wording and official Bulgarian government surfaces on caretaker status and office mechanics. Ambiguous evidence: contextual media and encyclopedic reporting on exact electoral strength and coalition viability.
sections.Evidence weighting: Highest weight went to contract mechanics, official government confirmation of caretaker status, official confirmation of Radev's resignation/succession, and independent contextual evidence that coalition math is difficult. I downweighted Wikipedia-style contextual sourcing and generic campaign rhetoric. I also downweighted the raw market signal somewhat because the public evidentiary trail did not independently justify near-certainty.
sections.Counterpoints / strongest disconfirming: The strongest disconfirming evidence against the below-market synthesis is the market itself at 0.9035 combined with multiple reports that Radev is not merely running but leading the main new electoral vehicle. If the market reflects reliable local coalition intelligence, the public-source skepticism here is too conservative.
sections.Resolution or source-of-truth interpreta: The contract is mostly clear. It resolves to the next person officially sworn in as PM after the election; caretaker/interim PMs do not count; official Government of Bulgaria information is primary; fallback is consensus credible reporting; if no qualifying PM is appointed by 2027-03-31 ET, resolution is Other. The main practical wrinkle is that the market price exists before the post-election government-formation sequence is complete, so traders can over-infer from electoral salience before formal swearing-in evidence exists.
sections.Why this could create or destroy alpha: This creates alpha only if the market is conflating 'Radev is the favorite' with 'Radev is almost certain.' Parliamentary coalition markets often punish that confusion. But alpha is fragile if local traders actually possess better coalition information than publicly accessible sources show. So the possible edge exists, but verification is not strong enough to treat it as an easy layup.
sections.What would falsify this interpretation /: This view would move sharply upward on multiple independent reports or official signals that key parties have agreed to back a Radev-led cabinet, or on a post-election seat map that makes his coalition path obviously dominant. It would move downward on explicit coalition vetoes, evidence of an alternative coalition focal PM, or signs that deadlock is likely enough to elevate Other materially.
sections.Highest-value next research: Immediately after the election, verify seat distribution and identify whether plausible coalition partners publicly commit to a Radev-led cabinet.
sections.Source-quality assessment: Most relied-on source class: contract language and official Bulgarian government/presidential surfaces for mechanics and status. Most important contextual source class: newswire and institutional-analysis reporting on Radev's candidacy and coalition fragmentation. Evidence independence is medium overall. Source-of-truth ambiguity is low on mechanics but medium on event-level probability before swearing-in. The synthesis is somewhat bottlenecked by thin upstream sourcing on explicit coalition commitments.
sections.Verification impact: Yes, additional verification beyond simple lane consolidation was used via cross-checking source-note artifacts. That materially increased confidence that Radev is a genuine contender and that caretaker exclusion is central, while also confirming that no lane had produced strong direct coalition-lock evidence. Cross-lane comparison reduced the credibility of the 0.35 base-rate tail but did not justify moving toward the market.
sections.Persona contribution map: base-rate — strongest challenge to near-certainty; highlighted fragmented-system outside view and Other/deadlock risk. catalyst-hunter — best framing of the decisive catalyst as post-election coalition formation rather than election day headlines. market-implied — best articulation of why the market is directionally coherent while still too aggressive on certainty. risk-manager — strongest provenance on Radev's resignation, candidacy reality, and fragmentation/coalition arithmetic. variant-view — best preserved the minority caution that public salience may be overweighted and that contract mechanics create more failure modes than the price implies.
sections.Reusable lesson signals: Durable lesson candidate: in parliamentary PM contracts, separate favorite status from formal investiture certainty. Underbuilt driver candidate: coalition-formation fragility may deserve more explicit treatment as a reusable driver. Source-quality lesson: very high market probabilities on rule-sensitive political contracts should trigger an explicit coalition-viability audit. Reusability confidence: medium.
sections.Orchestrator review suggestions: review later for durable lesson: yes; review later for driver candidate: yes; review later for canon or linkage issue: yes; review later for swarm-method issue: yes. Reason: this case exposed a recurring method issue where market confidence outpaces directly verified coalition evidence in parliamentary appointment markets.
sections.Recommended follow-up: Request a post-election refresh focused narrowly on official results, coalition math, and party commitment statements. If that is not possible yet, proceed with a below-market but medium-confidence view rather than a high-conviction anti-market stance.
